examples: There was dribble all down the baby's front.
Andy awoke the next morning in a pool of his own dribble, but ready to walk another day.
Steal: To take the ball away from the opposing team, either off the dribble or by picking off a pass.
The shot sneaked in under Enroth's left arm and had enough to dribble in behind him.
Many players stopped passing, trying instead to dribble all the way to the goal.
